workflow_description: Assesses whether strategic priorities, growth goals, and execution plans are clearly defined, owned, and translated into operating actions.
workflow_objective: strategic priorities are translated into funded initiatives, owners, milestones, and review routines
typical_evidence: planning calendar, priority register, and meeting pack or planning deck
kpi_examples: on-time planning cycle completion, initiative milestone attainment, and variance to plan

workflow_description: Assesses whether target customers, segments, buying needs, and commercial fit are clearly defined and used consistently across teams.
workflow_objective: the business targets the right customers and segments with clear qualification and value propositions
typical_evidence: ICP or segmentation model, qualification criteria, and customer taxonomy in CRM/ERP
kpi_examples: qualified lead rate by segment, win rate by segment, and revenue mix by segment

workflow_description: Assesses whether offers and services are clearly structured, scoped, documented, and operationally deliverable at the promised standard.
workflow_objective: offers are commercially viable, operationally deliverable, and clearly scoped before they are sold
typical_evidence: offer catalogue, service blueprint, and commercial rules
kpi_examples: gross margin by offer, delivery variance, and exception rate to standard

workflow_description: Assesses whether pricing models, margin assumptions, and commercial guardrails are intentionally designed and periodically reviewed.
workflow_objective: pricing reflects value, protects margin, and can be applied consistently across teams and systems
typical_evidence: price book, discount matrix, and approval matrix
kpi_examples: average discount to list, gross margin, and price realization

workflow_description: Assesses whether critical commercial and operational responsibilities are assigned, understood, and enforced across the business.
workflow_objective: teams know who decides, who executes, and who is accountable for operational outcomes
typical_evidence: RACI, org accountability map, and escalation matrix
kpi_examples: open issue aging, decision turnaround, and cross-functional handoff delays

workflow_description: Assesses whether leadership runs a regular operating cadence to review performance, decisions, risks, and execution priorities.
workflow_objective: leaders review performance, risk, and execution at the right rhythm and close actions quickly
typical_evidence: management calendar, standard agenda, and action log
kpi_examples: meeting adherence, action closure rate, and decision turnaround time

workflow_description: Assesses whether the business has reliable data, core KPIs, and reporting routines to support timely and informed decisions.
workflow_objective: management decisions are supported by trusted definitions, reliable reporting, and KPI ownership
typical_evidence: data dictionary, report catalogue, and KPI owner list
kpi_examples: report timeliness, data completeness, and reconciliation exceptions

workflow_description: Assesses whether the systems environment supports execution, control, visibility, integration, and scale readiness.
workflow_objective: core systems support the operating model with minimal duplicate entry, manual workarounds, or integration gaps
typical_evidence: application landscape, integration map, and system usage standards
kpi_examples: integration failure rate, system adoption, and manual workaround volume

workflow_description: Assesses whether marketing priorities, channel plans, budgets, and targets are intentionally set and linked to pipeline outcomes.
workflow_objective: marketing activity is aligned to targets, segments, budget, channels, and campaign objectives
typical_evidence: planning calendar, priority register, and meeting pack or planning deck
kpi_examples: on-time planning cycle completion, initiative milestone attainment, and variance to plan

workflow_description: Assesses whether campaigns are executed consistently, monitored effectively, and optimized against response and conversion goals.
workflow_objective: campaigns launch on time, follow standards, and generate measurable qualified response
typical_evidence: campaign brief, channel plan, and lead capture standards
kpi_examples: MQL volume, cost per qualified lead, and channel conversion

workflow_description: Assesses whether inbound demand is captured accurately, logged quickly, and made actionable for follow-up and reporting.
workflow_objective: all inbound enquiries and conversions are captured completely and routed quickly
typical_evidence: web forms, call capture script, and capture-to-CRM workflow
kpi_examples: speed to lead, capture completeness, and lead abandonment

workflow_description: Assesses whether outbound prospecting is targeted, repeatable, and managed with clear activity standards and response tracking.
workflow_objective: outbound activity targets the right accounts with consistent messaging and follow-up
typical_evidence: prospecting sequence, account list criteria, and activity standards
kpi_examples: connect rate, meeting booked rate, and outbound-sourced pipeline

workflow_description: Assesses whether leads are qualified against clear criteria so sales effort is focused on commercially viable opportunities.
workflow_objective: sales only receives leads that meet defined fit, need, and readiness criteria
typical_evidence: qualification framework, stage entry criteria, and rejection reason codes
kpi_examples: qualification-to-win rate, recycle rate, and accepted lead rate

workflow_description: Assesses whether qualified leads are routed to the right owner quickly and handed off without delay, duplication, or loss.
workflow_objective: qualified leads reach the right owner quickly with full context and SLA responsibility
typical_evidence: handoff template, acceptance checklist, and SLA rules
kpi_examples: handoff SLA attainment, handoff rejection rate, and rework volume

workflow_description: Assesses whether sales discovery and qualification are structured enough to validate need, fit, buying process, and deal quality early.
workflow_objective: opportunities are validated for pain, fit, authority, urgency, and commercial viability
typical_evidence: qualification framework, stage entry criteria, and rejection reason codes
kpi_examples: qualification-to-win rate, recycle rate, and accepted lead rate

workflow_description: Assesses whether opportunities are progressed through a defined pipeline with clear stage criteria, ownership, and visibility.
workflow_objective: opportunities move through the pipeline based on evidence, not optimism
typical_evidence: pipeline stages, deal review template, and next-step discipline rules
kpi_examples: stage conversion, sales cycle length, and forecast accuracy

workflow_description: Assesses whether proposals and quotes are accurate, timely, commercially sound, and aligned to approved scope and pricing.
workflow_objective: proposals and quotes are accurate, timely, competitive, and aligned to approved scope
typical_evidence: quote template, proposal checklist, and approval log
kpi_examples: quote turnaround time, proposal acceptance rate, and quote rework rate

workflow_description: Assesses whether discounts and pricing exceptions follow controlled approval rules that protect margin and commercial discipline.
workflow_objective: non-standard pricing is reviewed quickly while protecting margin and governance
typical_evidence: discount authority matrix, deal desk process, and margin threshold rules
kpi_examples: approval turnaround, average discount, and margin at approval

workflow_description: Assesses whether sellers gain access to the right decision-makers, influencers, and stakeholders needed to advance deals effectively.
workflow_objective: sales engages the right economic, technical, and user stakeholders early enough to reduce deal risk
typical_evidence: account map, stakeholder plan, and deal notes
kpi_examples: multi-threaded deal rate, win rate with decision-maker access, and late-stage loss reasons

workflow_description: Assesses whether follow-up activity is timely, systematic, and disciplined enough to maintain momentum and reduce deal slippage.
workflow_objective: buyers receive timely next steps, updates, and agreed actions throughout the cycle
typical_evidence: pipeline stages, deal review template, and next-step discipline rules
kpi_examples: stage conversion, sales cycle length, and forecast accuracy

workflow_description: Assesses whether sales leadership reviews pipeline health, deal risk, and conversion performance through a regular management cadence.
workflow_objective: risky deals are reviewed early with decisions, support, and escalation
typical_evidence: management calendar, standard agenda, and action log
kpi_examples: meeting adherence, action closure rate, and decision turnaround time

workflow_description: Assesses whether deals are closed through a controlled process that secures approvals, documentation, commitment, and next-step readiness.
workflow_objective: commercial, legal, procurement, and internal approvals are completed without avoidable last-minute blockage
typical_evidence: close plan, contract checklist, and approval status log
kpi_examples: close-plan completion, late-stage slip rate, and signature turnaround

workflow_description: Assesses whether sold work is transferred from sales into delivery with full context, clear scope, and agreed accountability.
workflow_objective: delivery receives complete commercial, scope, customer, and success context before mobilisation
typical_evidence: handoff template, acceptance checklist, and SLA rules
kpi_examples: handoff SLA attainment, handoff rejection rate, and rework volume

workflow_description: Assesses whether new customers or projects are onboarded in a structured way that supports readiness, adoption, and early success.
workflow_objective: new customers are activated quickly with clear plans, ownership, and adoption milestones
typical_evidence: onboarding plan, customer kickoff template, and go-live checklist
kpi_examples: time to go-live, time to first value, and onboarding completion rate

workflow_description: Assesses whether project work is planned, executed, tracked, and governed to meet scope, time, cost, and quality expectations.
workflow_objective: projects are delivered to agreed scope, quality, timeline, and commercial expectations
typical_evidence: delivery plan, project governance pack, and issue/risk register
kpi_examples: on-time delivery, budget variance, and defect/rework rate

workflow_description: Assesses whether ongoing service operations run consistently with clear workflows, workload control, and performance visibility.
workflow_objective: ongoing service requests are resolved predictably and at the expected experience level
typical_evidence: service catalogue, triage rules, and ticket workflow
kpi_examples: first response time, resolution SLA attainment, and repeat incident rate

workflow_description: Assesses whether people, time, and capacity are planned effectively to meet delivery demand without avoidable delays or overrun.
workflow_objective: the right capacity and skills are available when demand occurs
typical_evidence: capacity plan, skills matrix, and resource forecast
kpi_examples: utilization, resource fill rate, and capacity forecast accuracy

workflow_description: Assesses whether service levels are defined, monitored, and managed so commitments are met and issues are escalated early.
workflow_objective: service commitments are clearly defined, tracked, and recovered when missed
typical_evidence: SLA matrix, breach log, and service review pack
kpi_examples: SLA attainment, breach volume, and time to recover

workflow_description: Assesses whether quality checks, reviews, and corrective controls are embedded to protect delivery standards and customer outcomes.
workflow_objective: delivery quality is checked before defects reach customers or billing
typical_evidence: QA checklist, defect log, and approval gate
kpi_examples: defect rate, rework rate, and escaped defects

workflow_description: Assesses whether changes to scope, effort, or commercial terms are controlled so delivery and revenue remain aligned.
workflow_objective: scope, change requests, and commercial impacts are approved before work proceeds
typical_evidence: change request form, scope baseline, and approval log
kpi_examples: approved change rate, unbilled change value, and scope variance

workflow_description: Assesses whether billable events are clearly defined so earned revenue is identified consistently and without interpretation gaps.
workflow_objective: the business knows exactly which events, products, or services create a valid billing trigger
typical_evidence: billing rules, milestone acceptance criteria, and billable event register
kpi_examples: billable event capture rate, unbilled revenue aging, and trigger-to-invoice lead time

workflow_description: Assesses whether completed delivery, milestones, or usage trigger billing promptly and reliably without leakage between functions.
workflow_objective: completed work or billable milestones are converted into bill-ready signals immediately
typical_evidence: billing rules, milestone acceptance criteria, and billable event register
kpi_examples: billable event capture rate, unbilled revenue aging, and trigger-to-invoice lead time

workflow_description: Assesses whether usage, time, or other billable inputs are captured accurately and on time to support complete invoicing.
workflow_objective: all billable time, usage, and units are recorded completely and accurately
typical_evidence: timesheet policy, usage rules, and approval workflow
kpi_examples: timesheet compliance, usage capture completeness, and late-entry rate

workflow_description: Assesses whether billing rules, customer setups, and invoice prerequisites are maintained correctly before revenue is invoiced.
workflow_objective: customer, contract, tax, price, and billing rule setup is correct before invoices are produced
typical_evidence: billing setup form, contract details, and master data rules
kpi_examples: setup accuracy, invoice failure rate, and setup turnaround time

workflow_description: Assesses whether invoices are generated accurately, on schedule, and in line with contract terms and billable events.
workflow_objective: invoices are created on time, in full, and aligned to the commercial agreement
typical_evidence: invoice run calendar, exception queue, and invoice generation rules
kpi_examples: invoice-on-time rate, invoice error rate, and billing throughput

workflow_description: Assesses whether invoices are reviewed and released through appropriate controls that reduce billing error and delay.
workflow_objective: billing exceptions are resolved and invoices are approved without slowing cash
typical_evidence: invoice run calendar, exception queue, and invoice generation rules
kpi_examples: invoice-on-time rate, invoice error rate, and billing throughput

workflow_description: Assesses whether credit notes are raised, approved, and tracked under disciplined controls that protect revenue integrity.
workflow_objective: credits are tightly governed so genuine corrections are handled without uncontrolled margin leakage
typical_evidence: credit note policy, dispute log, and reason code taxonomy
kpi_examples: credit note value, dispute aging, and repeat dispute rate

workflow_description: Assesses whether billing disputes are resolved quickly and systematically to reduce rework, delay, and preventable revenue loss.
workflow_objective: billing disputes are resolved fast, root-caused, and prevented from recurring
typical_evidence: credit note policy, dispute log, and reason code taxonomy
kpi_examples: credit note value, dispute aging, and repeat dispute rate

workflow_description: Assesses whether customer credit risk is reviewed and controlled to protect cash flow and limit exposure to slow or failed payment.
workflow_objective: credit exposure is assessed, limits are maintained, and overdue risk is acted on early
typical_evidence: credit policy, limit register, and risk review log
kpi_examples: over-limit exposure, high-risk debtor balance, and credit-review completion

workflow_description: Assesses whether payment terms are set deliberately, approved appropriately, and aligned to commercial and risk objectives.
workflow_objective: approved payment terms are negotiated appropriately and recorded correctly in contracts and systems
typical_evidence: terms policy, contract clause standards, and system setup record
kpi_examples: average payment terms, terms exception rate, and cash-conversion impact

workflow_description: Assesses whether collections activity is timely, systematic, and visible enough to convert invoices into cash efficiently.
workflow_objective: overdue accounts are pursued consistently with structured action and escalation
typical_evidence: collections playbook, follow-up schedule, and aged debt tracker
kpi_examples: DSO, collections effectiveness index, and promise-to-pay kept rate

workflow_description: Assesses whether payment disputes are owned, resolved quickly, and prevented from stalling cash collection unnecessarily.
workflow_objective: payment blockers are resolved quickly across finance, sales, and operations
typical_evidence: dispute workflow, case log, and resolution SLA
kpi_examples: dispute aging, resolved disputes per period, and cash blocked by dispute

workflow_description: Assesses whether overdue accounts are escalated through clear rules and management action before debtor risk worsens.
workflow_objective: high-risk overdue accounts are escalated with clear authority and next-step decisions
typical_evidence: collections playbook, follow-up schedule, and aged debt tracker
kpi_examples: DSO, collections effectiveness index, and promise-to-pay kept rate

workflow_description: Assesses whether potential write-offs and bad debt exposure are reviewed early and managed with clear accountability.
workflow_objective: aged debt is reviewed objectively, provisioned appropriately, and fed back into prevention
typical_evidence: bad debt review pack, provision policy, and write-off approvals
kpi_examples: bad debt rate, recovery rate, and write-off aging

workflow_description: Assesses whether onboarding sets customers up effectively for adoption, value realization, and long-term retention.
workflow_objective: customers complete early setup and reach first value quickly
typical_evidence: onboarding plan, customer kickoff template, and go-live checklist
kpi_examples: time to go-live, time to first value, and onboarding completion rate

workflow_description: Assesses whether customers adopt the product or service as intended and reach the behaviors linked to commercial success.
workflow_objective: customers use the product or service deeply enough to receive ongoing value
typical_evidence: success plan, health score logic, and engagement plan
kpi_examples: time to value, adoption depth, and health score coverage

workflow_description: Assesses whether customer success ownership, review routines, and intervention mechanisms are in place to protect account health.
workflow_objective: customer health, risks, and opportunities are actively managed throughout the lifecycle
typical_evidence: success plan, health score logic, and engagement plan
kpi_examples: time to value, adoption depth, and health score coverage

workflow_description: Assesses whether renewals are forecast, prepared, negotiated, and secured through a controlled process before expiry risk builds.
workflow_objective: renewals are forecast, prepared, negotiated, and closed before contract expiry
typical_evidence: renewal calendar, at-risk renewal list, and commercial playbook
kpi_examples: on-time renewal start, gross renewal rate, and renewal forecast accuracy

workflow_description: Assesses whether churn risks are identified early and addressed through structured retention actions rather than reactive rescue.
workflow_objective: risk signals are spotted early and intervention plans are executed before loss occurs
typical_evidence: risk signal framework, save playbook, and risk review pack
kpi_examples: logo churn, save rate, and at-risk intervention lead time

workflow_description: Assesses whether at-risk customers are managed through defined save actions, decision rules, and commercial recovery options.
workflow_objective: at-risk customers receive structured save offers, executive intervention, or commercial remedies when appropriate
typical_evidence: risk signal framework, save playbook, and risk review pack
kpi_examples: logo churn, save rate, and at-risk intervention lead time

workflow_description: Assesses whether account plans exist for priority customers and guide retention, relationship management, and growth actions.
workflow_objective: top accounts have clear relationship maps, growth plans, and retention strategies
typical_evidence: account plan template, relationship map, and expansion tracker
kpi_examples: plan coverage for priority accounts, expansion pipeline, and net revenue retention

workflow_description: Assesses whether expansion, upsell, and cross-sell opportunities are identified, qualified, and progressed in a repeatable way.
workflow_objective: existing customers are converted into additional revenue through relevant offers and timing
typical_evidence: account plan template, relationship map, and expansion tracker
kpi_examples: plan coverage for priority accounts, expansion pipeline, and net revenue retention

workflow_description: Assesses whether finance operations support control, visibility, accuracy, and timely decision-making across the business.
workflow_objective: finance workflows support clean revenue, cost control, cash, and decision support
typical_evidence: finance SOPs, close or process checklist, and control logs
kpi_examples: process accuracy, close/process timeliness, and exception volume

workflow_description: Assesses whether staffing, skills, and capacity are planned and managed to support execution quality and growth.
workflow_objective: people capacity, skills, and accountability support the operating model
typical_evidence: workforce plan, skills matrix, and training plan
kpi_examples: vacancy fill time, skill coverage, and training completion

workflow_description: Assesses whether core technology and systems remain stable, secure, and fit for operational use without avoidable disruption.
workflow_objective: systems remain available, integrated, secure, and supportive of daily execution
typical_evidence: incident process, change calendar, and service health dashboard
kpi_examples: system uptime, incident resolution time, and repeat incident rate

workflow_description: Assesses whether data quality, reporting controls, and insight generation support trustworthy operational and commercial management.
workflow_objective: data is complete, accurate, timely, and trusted for operational decisions
typical_evidence: data dictionary, report catalogue, and KPI owner list
kpi_examples: report timeliness, data completeness, and reconciliation exceptions

workflow_description: Assesses whether approvals, delegations, and governance controls are clear enough to support disciplined decision-making.
workflow_objective: approval rights are clear and decisions happen without uncontrolled delay
typical_evidence: delegation of authority, approval workflow, and governance calendar
kpi_examples: approval turnaround, exception approvals, and policy breach incidents

workflow_description: Assesses whether key vendors and partners are selected, governed, and reviewed in a way that protects business performance.
workflow_objective: external partners deliver to expected quality, cost, and service levels
typical_evidence: vendor scorecard, contract/SOW, and service review notes
kpi_examples: vendor SLA attainment, cost variance, and vendor issue recurrence

workflow_description: Assesses whether risk, compliance, and control obligations are understood, monitored, and managed as part of normal operations.
workflow_objective: key operational, financial, legal, and security risks are controlled and evidenced
typical_evidence: risk register, control matrix, and compliance evidence pack
kpi_examples: control completion, breach count, and overdue remediation actions
